questions.text: Self-balancing trees, such as AVL trees, solve the degenerate-tree problem by...
choices: Refusing to insert a value that would make the tree unbalanced
Periodically restructuring the tree, rotating nodes, after insertions and deletions to keep the height close to log n
Always inserting new values at the root instead of at a leaf
questions.explanation: A self-balancing tree still accepts every value you insert, it just does extra work afterwards, rotating nodes around, to guarantee the height stays proportional to log n no matter what order the values arrive in. Refusing insertions or always rewriting the root are not how any standard self-balancing structure works.
